parts.answer: An answer that makes reference to the following points: • correct completion of top 2 boxes of Hess cycle • correct completion of bottom box of Hess cycle • inclusion of multiples of 2 for ∆ r H o [CH COOH(l)] and ∆ r H o [CH COONH (s)] • evidence of correct manipulation of Hess cycle to find ∆ r H o (products − reactants) • calculation of final answer (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Penalise omission/incorrect state symbols once only in M1 and M2 Allow H CO (aq) for H O(l) and CO (g) Example of calculation (2 x –586.3) and (2 x −484.5) Do not award if multiples applied to other enthalpy changes ∆ r H o = [((2x–586.3) + (−285.8) + (−393.5))] – [(2x–484.5) + (−939.9)] ∆ r H o = (+) 57 (kJ mol −1 ) Correct answer with no working scores M3, M4 and M5 −57( kJ mol −1 ) scores M3 and M5 (+)158.8 (kJ mol −1 ) scores M4 and M5 −158.8 (kJ mol −1 ) scores M5 Units if given must be correct. No TE from an incorrect Hess cycle but allow TE from M3 (5) Number Additional Guidance Mark
